results.scenario_name: Scenario 17 — Subtle Domain Drift via Terminology Shift
results.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Terminology collision identified explicitly — BR-4 and the escalation detail that 'confirmed' means paid+immutable for orders vs unpaid+verbal for quotes. (2) Distinct name recommended — 'accepted' or 'agreed' suggested with clear reasoning. (3) Payment-bypass risk identified — edge cases cover fulfillment triggering on unpaid quotes and support agents assuming payment occurred. (4) 3 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided for quote-to-order transition and terminology integrity. (5) Domain drift clearly explained throughout the analysis with business impact assessment.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough terminology collision analysis — BR-4 formalizes ubiquitous language consistency as an invariant
Strong recommendation for 'accepted' with multiple alternatives and clear rationale
Payment-bypass risk covered via edge cases: webhook/automation misfires, support confusion, reporting conflation
Correctly rejected the spec with a concrete rejection reason tied to the business rule violation
Edge cases are practical and production-relevant — support agent confusion, cross-entity reporting, automation misfires

Acceptance criteria focus on terminology integrity but lack explicit Given/When/Then for the quote-to-order transition with a payment step (e.g., 'Given an accepted quote, When payment is received, Then the order transitions to confirmed')
